#  Can we use different bank accounts to fund Procore Pay disbursements? 
#####  General Availability in Select Markets (United States)
![Flag Us](https://images.ctfassets.net/plvbd9i6qkpm/eb8f21ce49403a47d7bb5a0cedc7e0e9/7c0ec0977e654cc6bc9f3d8efa80fbe3/flag-us.png?fm=webp&h=14) [Procore Pay ](https://www.procore.com/pay) is available in the United States. It is designed for General Contractors and Owner-Builders who act as their own General Contractors on a job. Procore Pay extends the [Invoice Management](https://www.procore.com/invoice-management) functionality in the Procore web application to handle the payment process between general and specialty contractors.
## Answer
Yes. When payors operate multiple business divisions or have multiple checking accounts, add the account(s) to Procore Pay and configure them for each appropriate business entity. Then, choose a default funding account or let your team pick the account to use when [creating disbursements](https://v2.support.procore.com/process-guides/payments-admin-guide/create-disbursements). You’ll need to test each account added. You’ll also need to work with your bank to confirm if any prior authorizations must be in place.
Here's how:
  * Add bank accounts to use as funding sources: [Add Funding Accounts as a Payor](https://v2.support.procore.com/process-guides/about-the-payment-processing-tab-in-the-payments-tool/add-funding-accounts)
  * Set up bank accounts for each of your business entities in Procore Pay: [Add Business Entities as a Payor](https://v2.support.procore.com/process-guides/payor-setup-guide/add-entities)
  * Choose a default funding account or let your team pick the right account for each disbursement: [Customize the Payment Controls for Your Active Projects](https://v2.support.procore.com/product-manuals/procore-pay/tutorials/manage-payments-project-controls-for-a-single-project)
